stories.title: Metsola to rule on complaints about hate speech following deportations vote
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — European Parliament President Roberta Metsola has to decide whether to sanction two MEPs accused of hate speech after a vote last week on EU migration rules. Valérie Hayer, the head of the liberal Renew Europe group, complained in a letter to Metsola on Friday that Danish MEP Kristoffer Storm and Finnish MEP Sebastian Tynkkynen, both from the European Conservatives and Reformists group, had committed acts of “a racist nature” against Swedish MEP Abir Al-Sahlani, a bre

stories.title: Magyar unveils reform plans to write new constitution, oust top Orbán appointees
stories.summary: Hungarian Prime Minister Péter Magyar unveiled plans Monday to write a new constitution and oust several of Hungary’s top officials from his predecessor Viktor Orbán’s administration — including President Tamás Sulyok. In a nearly hour-long speech to parliament, Magyar announced “Operation Purifying Fire” to root out what he called the “Orbán mafia.” The proposed reforms could result in the removal of Sulyok, as well as the head of the country’s Supreme Court

stories.title: Ex-DUP chief found guilty of sexual abuse in Northern Ireland
stories.summary: NEWRY, Northern Ireland — The disgraced former leader of Northern Ireland’s Democratic Unionist Party, Jeffrey Donaldson, was found guilty Monday of committing a 23-year series of sexual crimes against two young victims. After three days of deliberations, a jury found Donaldson, 63, guilty on all 18 criminal counts that he had sexually abused two women when they were children. The charges included 13 counts of indecent assault, four of gross indecency, and one of rape. The jurors also found that

stories.title: Weather tracker: Extreme heat in Europe as typhoon strengthens off Philippines
stories.summary: Eastern Europe to see temperatures above early July average into next week The extreme heat experienced across central and western Europe, including the UK, will continue to shift eastwards. As slightly cooler weather infiltrates into western Europe, with risks of downpours and thunderstorms, eastern Europe is likely to see temperatures several degrees above the early July average into next week. Peak highs of between 35C and 40C are expected across southern Poland, Slovakia, Hungary and into th

stories.title: Forget closer EU ties if Tories can’t face up to Brexit truth | Letters
stories.summary: Dr Denis MacShane says Labour figures were willing to break ranks over Europe, and implores Conservatives to tell the truth about Brexit. Plus a letter from Zhengli Zhou Timothy Garton-Ash is right to be worried that Brexit Britain’s political class is now monolingual, with very little personal contact with deciders in the EU ( If the UK wants to rejoin the European Union, it first needs to understand it, 15 June ). He lists pro-EU forces in politics and polls, but it is an error to assume that

stories.title: Magyar announces "Operation Cleansing Fire"
stories.summary: The comprehensive political and legal effort is aimed at freeing Hungary from the grip of corruption according to the PM, who said that since his government has taken office, they keep finding more and more overpriced contracts and concessions the previous administration left behind.

stories.title: Mém lett abból, ahogy a FIFA tűzzel-vassal irtja a nemkívánatos márkákat a foci-vb-n
stories.summary: Még a ketchupos üvegeket is leragasztották fekete szalaggal, hogy ne látszódjon a márkájuk.

stories.title: Romania's Dan taps new PM, breaks democratic norms
stories.summary: President Nicusor Dan has nominated a new prime minister, without consulting Romania’s governing parties. Critics accuse Dan of breaking democratic rules.

stories.title: Az iráni válogatott az öltözőben hagyott írással üzent az amerikaiaknak és a világnak
stories.summary: A háború miatt csak különböző korlátozások mellett pályára lépő válogatott megköszönte Los Angelesnek a vendéglátást, és kiállt a béke mellett.

stories.title: Húsz éve még a csőd szélén állt, most a Samsungnál is értékesebb lett a dél-koreai csipgyártó cég
stories.summary: Ezzel Dél-Korea legértékesebb tőzsdén jegyzett vállalatává vált.

stories.title: 24 órás sztrájkot tart a cseh közmédia több ezer dolgozója a kormány tervei miatt
stories.summary: Andrej Babiš kormánya szigorúbban ellenőrizné a közmédiát, amivel évtizedek óta nem látott ellenállást váltott ki.

stories.title: Nem a pénzhiány a baj, hanem az intézményi bénultság: ezért marad le a magyar tudomány
stories.summary: Egy európai kutatási projekt elindítása Belgiumban körülbelül egy hónap volt. Ugyanez Magyarországon másfél évig tartott. Nem a pénz hiányzott, és nem is az emberek. A különbséget az intézményi működés adta. Ez a magyar tudomány egyik legnagyobb, mégis ritkán kimondott problémája.

stories.title: Jobban keresnek a gen Z dolgozók, mint a millenniálok az ő korukban egy brit agytröszt szerint
stories.summary: Az új kutatás megállapította, hogy jobb pénzügyi feltételek mellett kezdik a karrierjüket a Z generációsok, mint az Y generációsok. De nem biztos, hogy ez így is marad.

stories.title: A Lidl új programja helyi kezdeményezéseket támogat a vásárlók bevonásával
stories.summary: A Lidl éves adományozási programot indít Magyarországon: ezúttal nem termékekkel vagy stratégiai partnerségekkel, hanem közvetlen pénzügyi támogatással segítené a civil szervezetek munkáját. Az Érték.Lánc Program olyan helyi kezdeményezéseket keres, amik valós problémákra reagálnak, mérhető eredményeket hoznak, és hosszabb távon változást hoznak a közösségek életében.

stories.title: Magyar Péter Tisztítótűz-művelete, Trump iráni békéje és az első EU-csúcs Orbán nélkül – elemzés
stories.summary: Egész napos élő adás az amerikai–iráni békekötés folyamatáról, Magyar Péter első EU-csúcsának tanulságairól és a rendkívüli parlamentről – a Telex újságíróinak elemzésével.

stories.summary: „Az iszapban járás kimerítő, és nem is túl gusztusos” – írta a tó átsétálásáról Kürti Gábor.

stories.title: Moving on from the sins of the Orbán regime too quickly would serve the interests of the perpetrators – Péter Krekó
stories.summary: More and more former servants of the Orbán system have been speaking out, but we haven’t heard nearly enough about the victims’ perspective, according to Péter Krekó. An interview with the political and behavioural scientist about the importance of Hungarian society coming to terms with the trauma of the past sixteen years.

stories.title: Magyar Péter a maffiáról és a Tisztítótűz-műveletről beszélt a rendkívüli parlamenti ülésen
stories.summary: A miniszterelnök átfogó akciót hirdetett „a politikai és gazdasági maffia” felszámolására. Az Alaptörvény módosításával elmozdítanák Sulyok Tamást és Polt Pétert is. Gulyás Gergely szerint Magyar nem maffiával küzd, hanem diktatúrát épít. Napirend előttiek, percről percre.

stories.summary: A Tisztítótűz művelet részeként Sulyok Tamás elmozdítását is kezdeményezi a kormány.

stories.summary: A Tisztítótűz művelet értelmében Szijjártónak, Lázárnak, Gulyásnak és Kocsisnak is búcsúznia kellene a parlamenttől, míg a 70 éves korlimit négy alkotmánybírót érintene.

stories.title: Magyar röviden ismertette, hogyan működik majd a Nemzeti Vagyonvisszaszerzési és Védelmi Hivatal
stories.summary: A miniszterelnök szerint akár az MNB-alapítványok ügyét is magához vonhatja majd a hivatal más hatóságoktól.

stories.title: Magyar Péter: Sulyok Tamás egy hónap múlva már nem lesz Magyarország köztársasági elnöke
stories.summary: Ha Sulyok nem írja alá az Alaptörvény módosítását, kezdeményezik a megfosztási eljárást vele szemben, és az eljárás alatt már az Országgyűlés elnöke gyakorolja a jogait. A miniszterelnök szerint augusztus 20-a előtt megválasztja az Országgyűlés az új köztársasági elnököt, és úgy tudja, hogy a legfőbb ügyész hamarosan lemond.

stories.title: A vízminőség alapján nem Albániában érdemes strandolni
stories.summary: Bár az ország egyre felkapottabb turisztikai célpont, a strandok vízminőségével komoly gondok vannak. Cipruson és Szlovéniában a legjobb a helyzet a magyarok által is kedvelt nyaralóhelyek közül.

stories.title: Észak Királya – Keir Starmer az ő miniszterelnöksége útjából állt félre
stories.summary: Egy időközi választást megnyerve lett parlamenti képviselő Manchester polgármestere, akit messiásként vártak a brit Munkáspártban. Andy Burnham és gondosan válogatott ruhatára most az átlagemberek érdekeit képviselő, elitellenes politikusként visszatért, hogy átvegye az Egyesült Királyság irányítását.

stories.title: I challenge the Rothko naysayers to stand in front of his monumental art and not feel awe – Rhiannon Lucy Cosslett
stories.summary: An exhibition in Florence that pairs his giant canvases with Renaissance religious art brought me to the edge of tears. It is the perfect refuge from the infinite scroll As an unbaptised agnostic raised with no religion, the closest I ever really come to a spiritual experience is when I’m standing in front of an artwork. Last week I went to Florence to do exactly that, drawn there not by Michelangelo’s David or Botticelli’s Birth of Venus, but by the works of Mark Rothko, that titan of US abstra

stories.title: Zelenskyy returns Poland's highest honor as row deepens
stories.summary: Ukraine's president has returned a medal to Poland after a decision to strip him of the country's highest honor. Warsaw has been one of Kyiv's key allies since Russia began its war in Ukraine.

stories.title: Poland: Arrest after Russian artist and Putin critic killed

stories.title: Russian dissident artist Semyon Skrepezki murdered in Poland
stories.summary: Skrepezki was known for his scathing caricatures of Russian President Vladimir Putin. His suspected killer has been apprehended.

stories.title: Czech government causes alarm with plan to scrap license fee
stories.summary: The Czech government has approved a bill to put the funding of public service media under direct political control. Critics warn that if passed, the bill would put a pillar of Czech democracy at risk.

stories.summary: The Russian frigate fired warning shots near a British retired couple on a yacht in the English Channel.

stories.title: Ukraine intensifies attacks on Crimea to raise cost of Russian occupation
stories.summary: Volodymyr Zelenskyy says strikes on oil facilities part of ‘long-range sanctions’ intended to isolate the territory Ukraine has stepped up its strikes on Crimea as part of a strategy to isolate the occupied peninsula from mainland Russia and raise the cost of the occupation. On Sunday, Russian-installed authorities suspended civilian fuel sales until at least Wednesday, a move that underscored Ukraine’s growing ability to disrupt supply lines linking Crimea to Russia. stories.summary: European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen was among the first EU leaders to react to British Prime Minister Keir Starmer’s resignation announcement on Monday. In a warm message thanking “dear Keir,” the head of the EU’s executive praised the British leader for the accomplishments achieved during his less than two years in office. “It can take many leaders years to grow into the statesman you became in just two years,” wrote von der Leyen on X. stories.summary: Keir Starmer in a Croatia jersey. An Iranian protest against a US strike. Calls to arrest Brazil's President Lula. Adolf Hitler in the German fan section — all circulating around the World Cup. But none of it is real.

Of the six prime ministers that have led Britain over the last decade, with a seventh now on the way, it will be the fall of Keir Starmer that will most perplex the political analysts of the future. The public has been gripped by the ill-fated $14m bid to renovate the reflecting pool, which the US president vowed to make “beautiful” in time for this summer’s birthday celebrations at the capital.

stories.title: Big gas is trying to cash in on Europe’s fossil fuel crisis
stories.summary: As Europe grapples with spiking energy prices and debates how to strengthen its energy system, one issue has become the wrong target of criticism: methane rules. The EU Methane Regulation decreases gas waste by requiring fossil fuel companies to monitor, report and reduce methane emissions — the second most potent greenhouse gas after CO2. It is the world’s first binding legislation of its kind, applying not only to domestic producers but also to imported fossil fuels. If fully implemented, it c

stories.title: Iran-US peace talks show ‘encouraging progress,’ say mediators
stories.summary: Qatari and Pakistani mediators said on Monday that negotiations on a U.S.-Iran peace deal showed “encouraging progress” and that talks will continue at a technical level. The statement came after the U.S., Iran and the two mediating countries concluded talks in Switzerland, which marked the first high-level meeting between Washington and Tehran since the start of the war in late February. The two countries signed a preliminary agreement last week aimed at ending hostilities, opening

stories.title: EU-UK summit postponed as Keir Starmer resigns
stories.summary: LONDON — The EU-U.K. summit planned for next month will be postponed, the president of the European Council has said. António Costa said he hoped that Starmer’s successor as prime minister would give “continuity” to the plans to reset the cross-channel relationship. The meeting was meant to take place on July 22 to conclude talks on an agrifood agreement, an emissions trading deal, and a youth mobility scheme. stories.title: Los Angeles schools superintendent resigns after FBI search warrants

Alberto Carvalho had been put on leave after news of the federal investigation. stories.title: Brown skuas and giant petrels rarely make landfall. When they were found in WA, scientists feared ‘bad news’ for wildlife
stories.summary: Experts thought H5N1 bird flu would more likely reach Australia’s north. But an arrival from Antarctica had always been possible Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Brown skuas and giant petrels are a common sight offshore in southern Australian waters in the winter months, but they will rarely risk venturing on to land. So when two of these birds were discovered sick – on beaches a few kilometres apart on Western Australia’s southern coastline – it was a sign something

stories.title: Unhappy camper: man rescued after falling into toilet at California campsite
stories.summary: Unidentified man fell into vault toilet at Camp Edison while trying to retrieve sunglasses he dropped, officials say An unhappy camper spent about 15 minutes submerged in sewage in the putrid tank of a California campground’s vault toilet after falling in trying to retrieve sunglasses he dropped, according to officials. The latest entry into the annals of bizarre US campground mishaps took place on Saturday at Shaver Lake’s Camp Edison, about 50 miles north-east of downtown Fresno. A spokesperso

stories.title: Two children found dead in car in France as heatwave hits Europe
stories.summary: Number of countries issue alerts with sustained and rising temperatures expected to present danger to health Europe live – latest updates Two children aged four and two have been found dead in their family’s car in south-eastern France, the local prosecutor said, as a large swathe of western Europe suffers a ferocious heatwave forecast to shatter absolute temperature records. “The causes of death are yet to be determined, but the heat is the leading line of inquiry,” said Hélène Mourges, the pro

stories.title: British man accused of conspiring to drug and rape wife over two decades admits to a dozen sexual offences
stories.summary: Guilty pleas span three-year period, including sexually assaulting her with another man in 2024 A man accused of conspiring with others to drug and rape his unconscious wife has admitted sexually assaulting her over a period of three years. The man, in his 60s, pleaded guilty to two counts of rape and 10 sexual offences at Minshull Street crown court in Manchester . Continue reading...

stories.title: Far-right millionaire wins Colombia’s razor-tight presidential election
stories.summary: Leftwing opponent alleges vote count irregularities after Trump-endorsed lawyer Abelardo de la Espriella secures narrow majority A Trump-admiring far-right millionaire lawyer and self-styled “outsider” has defeated a leftwing senator by a razor-thin margin to win Colombia’s presidential runoff, in an election that promises to mark a dramatic shift in the country’s decades-long armed conflict. With 99.99% of ballots counted in the preliminary vote tally, the far-right lawyer Abelardo de la Esprie

stories.title: US-Iran talks strained as Trump threats spark Iranian walkout
stories.summary: Talks expected to continue for rest of the week despite disruption caused by US president’s threat to bomb Iran and kidnap negotiating team High-stakes talks between the US and Iran are expected to continue for the rest of the week in Switzerland, after a tense start that saw Iranian negotiators walk out in protest at a stream of threats issued by Donald Trump on social media. The US president had threatened to bomb Iran and even to kidnap the Iranian negotiating team unless the strait of Hormuz

stories.title: Germany news: Could heatwave temperatures hit 40°C?
stories.summary: Germany is in the grip of an intense heatwave, with experts predicting extreme temperatures in some regions. Chancellor Friedrich Merz insists he can handle the heat and is not bothered by personal insults. DW has more.

stories.title: How TikTok-style videos keep human brains hooked on content
stories.summary: TikTok, Instagram and YouTube clips are designed to overwhelm the brain's pleasure circuitry and keep people watching. Researchers say short-form videos may only be the start when it comes to harvesting human attention.

stories.title: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer announces resignation
stories.summary: In an emotional speech at 10 Downing Street, Keir Starmer said he will step down after days of mounting pressure and speculation over his future. It comes as leadership contender Andy Burnham returns to parliament.

stories.summary: More than 2 million students appeared for the high-stake NEET medical entrance exam after the question paper leak fiasco amid high security. Follow DW for more.

stories.title: Germany's Left Party is dreaming big

stories.title: Former US Fed chair Alan Greenspan dies aged 100
stories.summary: Alan Greenspan oversaw the US Federal Reserve for nearly two decades from 1987 until 2006. Critics argue that his policies contributed to the Global Financial Crisis of 2008.

stories.title: The man who knew, but didn't say: Alan Greenspan (1926-2026)
stories.summary: Alan Greenspan’s reputation as head of the US Federal Reserve was legendary — not least because of his reputation for convoluted statements. However, the 2008/9 financial crisis took a toll on his standing.

stories.summary: For 75 years, the Goethe-Institut has stood for cultural exchange, education and partnerships around the globe — even under challenging political conditions.

stories.title: How inflation undermines Nigeria's sugar tax

stories.title: US-Iran deal: Pakistan's diplomatic masterstroke?
stories.summary: By stepping in as mediator in the Iran war, Pakistan has managed to elevate its status from a regional player to a global diplomatic stakeholder — with a little help from China and its friends in the Gulf.

stories.summary: While both sides claim "encouraging progress," experts say there's a long way to go to turn the shaky diplomatic framework into a durable agreement.

stories.title: Germany's pension plans draw praise and outrage

stories.title: Canada’s policies force asylum seekers into US to face deportation, critics say

stories.summary: Abiy Ahmed's party retains its huge majority despite unrest in several parts of Ethiopia and tensions with its neighbours.

stories.title: The week around the world in 20 pictures
stories.summary: Ukrainian strikes on a Moscow oil refinery, protests at the G7 summit, wildfires in Spain and Messi at the World Cup – the past seven days as captured by the world’s leading photojournalists Warning: this gallery contains images some readers may find distressing Continue reading...

stories.title: Ghana conference calls for formal apology for transatlantic slave trade
stories.summary: Global framework for reparatory justice adopted at event includes demand for compensation and debt relief More than money: the logic of slavery reparations A global framework for reparatory justice has been adopted at a conference in Ghana, as African and Caribbean leaders demanded formal apologies from countries that benefited from the transatlantic slave trade. Heads of state and government and other officials formally approved the strategy on Friday at a gathering in a hotel in the capital, A

stories.title: Taliban order ban on smartphones as officials shown destroying devices
stories.summary: Directive aimed at government workers, but reports of wider implementation spark warnings of future Afghanistan-wide prohibition The Taliban have ordered a sweeping ban on the use of smartphones by government officials – in what some analysts say could foreshadow broader, population-level restrictions. In a directive issued by the Taliban’s military courts and reviewed by the Guardian, the ban was to take effect this week and prohibits “high rank, low rank, general mujahideen, or service staff”

stories.title: German journalist detained in Syria back home, lawyer says
stories.summary: Eva Maria Michelmann vanished in Syria in January as government forces advanced on Kurdish fighters in the northeast. The German government last week said it was working to get her home.

stories.title: EU ban on BPA chemical in food packaging takes effect
stories.summary: From July 2026 onwards, BPA will no longer be permitted in EU food packaging. The industrial chemical is known to interfere with the human hormonal system.

stories.title: Inequality is elementary to Germany's school system
stories.summary: Children's paths through Germany's school system are often determined before their first day of kindergarten, according to the national report on education. An elementary school in Bonn is offering a new model for kids.

stories.title: Would you choose to take a 22-hour non-stop flight?
stories.summary: The BBC asked Sydney locals if they would take the newly announced, longest ever commercial flight from Sydney to London.

stories.title: Japanese pop group XG went from brutal five year training to global pop stars
stories.summary: The Japanese pop group have become global stars, after being recruited before they were teenagers.

stories.title: African and Caribbean nations call for formal apology for transatlantic slavery
stories.summary: The leaders have asked for apologies from the countries that benefited from the slave trade, as well as debt relief and financial compensation.

stories.title: Steve Rosenberg: Moscow oil refinery attack brings Russia's war with Ukraine closer to home
stories.summary: There are moments when life in Moscow feels completely normal - Thursday morning wasn't one of them, writes Steve Rosenberg.

stories.title: BBC sees destroyed villages in Israeli-occupied southern Lebanon
stories.summary: Travelling with a humanitarian convoy, BBC's Hugo Bachega has been given rare access to a part of Lebanon under Israeli occupation.

stories.title: Rightwing MEPs chant 'send them back' after controversial deportation plans are passed – video
stories.summary: Several lawmakers celebrated a vote in the European parliament approving plans to increase deportations of undocumented people. Chants of 'send them back' spurred other MEPs to chant back 'shame on you'. The plans could lead to ICE-style immigration measures being implemented in Europe, which have been criticised by rights groups Continue reading...

stories.title: Vietnam police rescue hundreds of cats stolen for meat by crime ring
stories.summary: Major operation launched after spate of pet thefts in Ho Chi Minh City, according to local media Police in Vietnam have rescued more than 400 cats in a bust of a cat meat crime ring in Ho Chi Minh City, according to animal welfare groups and local media reports. More than 40 cats were reunited with their owners after the multiday operation last week, but several dozen of those rescued have died due to the harsh conditions in which they were found, the groups said. Continue reading...

stories.title: ‘Period tax’ on sanitary products to be abolished, says Pakistan minister
stories.summary: Campaigners welcome announcement cutting levies on menstrual health items, but say their work to end period poverty is ‘far from over’ Pakistan plans to abolish “period tax”, in a victory for young campaigners who had taken the government to court over the charges. Finance minister Muhammad Aurangzeb announced that sanitary towels and related items were “daily necessities that are indispensable for women’s health, dignity and full participation in social activities”, and said he intended to remo

stories.title: Barbados prime minister announces manifesto for slavery reparations
stories.summary: Updated document, which emphasises harm done to African women, is being considered by other Caribbean countries Barbados’s prime minister, Mia Mottley, has announced a new manifesto from Caribbean leaders asserting the “moral, ethical and legal case” for reparations over damage caused by hundreds of years of enslavement. Mottley was speaking at a “historic” conference in Ghana to advance the push for reparatory justice after the United Nations adopted a landmark resolution declaring the traffick

stories.title: Israel says it's cutting ties to EU's Kaja Kallas
stories.summary: Israel's foreign minister says he is severing contact with the EU's foreign affairs chief Kaja Kallas. Gideon Saar attributed the move to a media report alleging that Kallas compared Israel to apartheid-era South Africa.

stories.title: Drones create the first-ever Fifa scoreboard in Seattle sky
stories.summary: BBC reporter Max Matza is in Seattle, Washington, where 400 drones lit up the night sky on Monday to display the score of the Egypt v Belgium match.

stories.title: Thousands killed in US-Israeli war on Iran - but experts say true total may never be known
stories.summary: Experts say internet, media and government restrictions have all hampered casualty reporting across the region.

stories.title: Lululemon apologises after Japanese drum row at Great Wall yoga event
stories.summary: Online uproar follows Canadian brand’s use of taiko drum at sponsored festival held to celebrate Chinese culture The activewear brand Lululemon has apologised after a promotional event held on the Great Wall of China appeared to mistakenly feature a Japanese drum, prompting an uproar. The Canadian-headquartered company, known for its upmarket leggings, has been growing rapidly in China and arranged for a yoga festival to take place in late May on a section of the wall near Beijing. Continue read

stories.title: South African men sentenced in ‘world’s largest’ rhino horn trafficking case
stories.summary: ‘Mastermind’ Dawie Groenewald given fine of 2m rand or four-year jail term almost 16 years after arrest Two traffickers of rhino horns have been sentenced by a South African court in what police said was the world’s largest such case, partly bringing to an end an almost two-decade legal saga. Dawie Groenewald and Tielman Erasmus had faced more than 1,700 charges ranging from illegally hunting and dehorning rhinos to racketeering and money laundering. Continue reading...

stories.title: Weather tracker: Severe thunderstorms sweep Europe and east Asia
stories.summary: Strong winds and heavy rain batter Slovenia, while France experiences atypical heatwave Severe thunderstorms swept across the Balkans last week, bringing widespread destruction to parts of the region. The storms developed as unstable hot air lingered over the Adriatic Sea while a cold front plunged south-eastward. The front began its journey on 10 June in Slovenia, where the Slovenian Environment Agency recorded 65mph gusts at Ljubljana airport. Heavy rain also fell widely across the region with

stories.title: 'Neither I nor Italy ever beg': Giorgia Meloni rejects Trump's claim she begged for a photo – video
stories.summary: The Italian prime minister posted a video on X saying that Donald Trump's claim she begged him for a photo was 'completely made up'. The US president made the comment to Italy’s La7 TV channel after the G7 summit in France, saying: 'She wanted a picture with me so badly. I wouldn’t have taken it, but I felt sorry for her' Italy PM Meloni ‘stunned’ by Trump’s claims she begged him for a photo Continue reading...

stories.title: Nathaniel Brown: The left back Germany have been waiting for
stories.summary: A strong option at left back is overdue for Germany. Nathaniel Brown is quickly becoming a star at the World Cup.

stories.title: Berlin summit brings out private equity protesters
stories.summary: Activists gathered to protest the SuperReturn private equity summit in Berlin, saying the industry drives inequality, job cuts and rising costs as investors representing trillions of Euros gather to seek high returns.

stories.title: How a feel-good German song is taking over social media
stories.summary: Being encouraging and building confidence is what the German song "Gut genug" ("Good Enough") is all about. A meme is now bringing the track and its message to millions of people around the world.

stories.title: Strait of Hormuz: German military prepares for possible deployment
stories.summary: Following the tentative deal with the US, Iran is planning to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. The German Bundeswehr is planning to assist with the mine-clearing.

stories.title: Germany's 2026 World Cup squad built on African roots
stories.summary: Germany's 2026 World Cup squad has leaders with African heritage. The diversity of the team is a strength.

stories.title: US curbs Anthropic AI access, raising global concerns
stories.summary: The Trump administration has restricted foreign access to Anthropic's latest AI models over security fears, forcing a global shutdown. Could the row hurt the Claude-maker's IPO plans and set a far-reaching precedent?

stories.title: New exhibitions for Anish Kapoor: Art on the edge
stories.summary: His art challenges our perception: Forms vanish and perspectives shift. Anish Kapoor leads the viewer to the edge of the abyss — and sometimes, even beyond. Two new exhibitions revisit his work.

stories.title: Women tackling coal dust and other eco wins this week
stories.summary: Indian women fight back against toxic coal dust, the US hits a solar milestone, and there's good news for soil in Kenya and cars worldwide.

stories.title: Can Europe build its own AI heavyweights to rival the US?
stories.summary: With AI dominated by US firms, Europe is pushing for more independence. New partnerships, investments and startups aim to boost the continent's digital sovereignty, but challenges around funding and regulation remain.

stories.title: As heat waves grow more frequent and fierce, cities worldwide are racing to cool streets and reshape urban life. But can they adapt quickly enough?
stories.summary: As heat waves grow more frequent and fierce, cities worldwide are racing to cool streets and reshape urban life.
